title: The ratio machine
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Make the geometric construction of a/b feel like a usable instrument.
prompt_core: Illustrate the ratio machine with two axes, a point representing a, a point representing b, a connector line, and a parallel through the unit point producing the ratio output. Stage it as a beautifully lit instrument on vellum, almost like a scientific device for extracting pure ratios from magnitudes. Clean geometric scaffolding, enough atmosphere to feel memorable, and room for precise overlays.
caption: A ratio can be constructed geometrically rather than memorized symbolically.
overlay: Exact axes, parallels, and labels should be overlaid after generation.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.
api_prompt:

The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

Project context:
This image belongs to a rigorous adult mathematics book for neurodivergent visual thinkers.
It should function as a cognitive bridge for subsection "7.1 The ratio machine".
Treat it as editorial art with pedagogical duty.

Image role:
Make the geometric construction of a/b feel like a usable instrument.

Layout intent:
D — Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed

Style direction:
ratio instrument plate

Core image brief:
Illustrate the ratio machine with two axes, a point representing a, a point representing b, a connector line, and a parallel through the unit point producing the ratio output. Stage it as a beautifully lit instrument on vellum, almost like a scientific device for extracting pure ratios from magnitudes. Clean geometric scaffolding, enough atmosphere to feel memorable, and room for precise overlays.

Caption intent for layout reference only:
A ratio can be constructed geometrically rather than memorized symbolically.

Overlay / production note:
Exact axes, parallels, and labels should be overlaid after generation.

Rendering instructions:
Do not place explanatory typography, equations, or labels inside the art unless they are intrinsic physical objects in the scene.
Preserve clear compositional space for later book-layout overlays where appropriate.
Make the concept immediately graspable to an intelligent adult who distrusts rote schooling and learns through spatial pattern, tactile intuition, and elegant structure.
Keep the mood intellectually serious, lucid, and memorable rather than cute.

Negative constraints:
No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

title: What reciprocal actually means
role: Replace the ritual word reciprocal with the lived idea of undo-scaling.
prompt_core: Show a segment or image first enlarged by a scale factor and then returned exactly to its original size by the inverse scale, with the second action visually coded as the precise undoing of the first. The scene could use a zoom lens, camera crop, or optical projector returning to baseline. The reader should feel the reciprocal as an inverse transformation, not a numerator-denominator flip chant.
caption: A reciprocal is the scale that undoes a scale.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. source_key: 7.3 Why dividing by a fraction becomes multiplying by its reciprocal
title: Why dividing by a fraction becomes multiplying by its reciprocal
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Derive the famous rule from inverse actions rather than slogan repetition.
prompt_core: Compose a clear sequential image: a fractional scale factor acts on a magnitude, then an inverse scale restores the unit, revealing why division by that fraction must be multiplication by its undoing factor. The geometry can be hybrid—part ratio machine, part transformation sequence. The important feeling is inevitability: the algorithm falls out of inverse structure. Precise but visually warm.
caption: The slogan is a consequence of inverse scaling, not the starting point.
overlay: Overlay symbols after generation if desired.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

source_key: 7.4 Another lens: zoom factors on a screen
title: Another lens: zoom factors on a screen
role: Offer a modern intuitive analogy for ratio and reciprocal.
prompt_core: Show a sophisticated digital zoom interface or camera monitor moving from 100% to 200% and back down to 50%, with the visual emphasis on how one zoom undoes another. The interface should be abstracted and elegant, not brand-specific. The analogy should let an adult learner feel reciprocal scaling in their bones through a familiar modern action.
caption: Zoom factors make inverse scaling intuitive.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. title: Thought experiments
role: Invite the learner to feel the ratio machine as an action sequence.
prompt_core: Depict a hand moving along a simple ratio instrument, sliding from a known magnitude to the unit point and watching a pure ratio emerge on the other axis. Keep it sparse, like a designer’s sketch of a conceptual tool. The image should invite mental rehearsal of 'what action do I perform, and what kind of thing comes out?'
caption: Think in terms of the operation performed, not only the final quotient.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame.
